description_long: This object is a rectangular, terracotta-colored stone block with two deep rectangular slots on its top surface, suggesting it may serve as a divider or holder. The block features a prominent, protruding rectangular ledge on one side, which could function as a handle or a receptacle for holding or supporting items. The surfaces are matte and slightly uneven, with visible hand-crafted texture and minor imperfections along the edges, giving it a rustic appearance. The block is solid except for the open slots, which penetrate deeply into the body, and the ledge is hollowed out. The color is a consistent warm, earthy orange, typical of fired clay or stone. There are no visible engravings, markings, or significant weathering, and the block appears robust and heavy.
description: A terracotta-colored stone block with two deep top slots and a protruding rectangular ledge, featuring a rustic, hand-crafted texture.
description_view_0: Front view shows the two deep top slots and the prominent rectangular ledge protruding from the face.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the solid side of the block and the depth of the top slots, with the ledge visible extending from the opposite side.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the block's back face with the ledge visible, and the two top slots clearly defined.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the solid side and the ledge extending from the far side, with the top slots visible.
attribute_installation_method: set in sand or placed on pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear clean with minor edge imperfections typical of hand-crafted stone.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or used as a modular divider
